text.content:
Respite: Player Guide

Long rests in Respite are more than clicking a button. Where the party camps, what gear they carry, and what they choose to do during the night all affect how much they recover.

How it works

Each rest runs through four phases: Setup (the GM sets terrain and shelter), Activities (each character picks a camp activity), Events (the GM rolls for encounters), and Resolution (HP, hit dice, spell slots, and exhaustion are processed). If meal tracking is on, there is also a rations step where characters need food and water.

Comfort

Comfort is the single biggest factor in how much the party recovers. The GM sets it based on the environment, but shelter, fire, and certain activities can shift it up or down.

TierHPHit DiceExhaustion
SafeFullFullNo encounter risk
ShelteredFullFullNormal recovery
RoughFull-1 HDCON DC 10 or +1
Hostile75%-2 HDCON DC 15 or +1

Gear that matters

Respite checks the party's inventory for camping gear. If a character has it, the module detects it automatically.

ItemEffect
Bedroll+1 Hit Die recovered during long rest
Mess KitAdvantage on exhaustion saves (requires a lit campfire)
TentShelter option that cancels or reduces weather penalties

Bedrolls and mess kits are cheap and light. A tent is heavier (20 lb), so carrying one is a real choice. Forgetting any of them in the wild has consequences.

Activities

Each character picks one camp activity. There is a tension between helping the camp and helping yourself. Guarding lowers the chance of an event but means less personal recovery. Resting fully boosts comfort by one tier but leaves the character in deep sleep if something goes wrong.

Some activities are shared duties (Keep Watch, Scout, Set Up Defenses, Tend Wounds). Others are personal (Rest Fully, Training, Forage, Fletch Arrows). Spellcasters may also see arcane options like Identify Items, Attune Item, or Copy Spell.

Campfire

Someone needs to tend the fire. Fire level affects comfort and encounter odds. A bonfire boosts comfort by one step but is visible from a distance. An unlit camp drops comfort by one step. Drag kindling from inventory to fuel it, or whittle to contribute without spending items.

Events

The night is not always safe. The GM rolls against the encounter DC, which is shaped by terrain, weather, shelter, and what the party did during activities. Events can be hostile (creatures, hazards), complications (gear damage, resource loss), or discoveries (useful finds). Sometimes nothing happens. Sometimes it does.

The party's choices during activities directly affect these odds. More guards and scouts mean a safer night.

Launching a Rest

Only the GM can start a rest. Two ways to do it:

  • Chat command: type /respite in the chat bar.
  • Scene control: click the campground icon in the token controls toolbar (left side of the canvas).

The module intercepts long rests by default. When a player clicks "Long Rest" on their character sheet, Respite opens instead of the standard dialog. This can be toggled off in settings.

Module Settings

All settings are under Game Settings > Module Settings > Ionrift Respite.

  • Default Comfort Level - The comfort tier used when the GM does not pick one during setup.
  • Intercept Player Rests - When enabled, clicking "Long Rest" on a character sheet opens the Respite flow instead of the default dialog.
  • Armor Doff/Don Advisory - Shows a reminder when characters are resting in heavy armor (Xanathar's rules).
  • Study Activity - Enables the Study activity tile for identifying items, copying spells, and Detect Magic scans.
  • Track Food & Water - Enables the rations phase. When off, the rest skips straight from Activities to Events.
  • Partial Sustenance - Allows partial food/water (half rations) without full penalties.
  • Content Packs - Enable or disable expansion event packs. Shows event counts per terrain.

Terrains

Terrain sets the event pool and baseline encounter DC. Available terrains include forest, desert, swamp, coast, arctic, underdark, urban, ruins, dungeon, and tavern. Each has its own events and base difficulty.

Events

Events are pulled from terrain-tagged pools with weighted selection. They never repeat until the pool cycles. Each event resolves on a 4-tier scale (triumph, success, mixed, failure) based on how far above or below the DC the roll lands.

The base pack ships with events across all terrains. Additional event packs can be enabled through the Content Packs settings panel.

Event Browser

To browse all available events, open Module Settings > Content Packs > Manage Packs, then click Browse Events at the bottom of the panel. The browser lets you filter by terrain and page through every event with its full outcome tiers.

Event Categories

CategoryWhat it means
EncounterSomething hostile or confrontational. May lead to combat.
ComplicationA problem that needs solving. Skill checks, resource loss, condition risks.
DiscoverySomething useful found. Items, information, or resources.
AmbientColour and atmosphere. No mechanical impact, sets the tone.
